Title: Fahmi urges firms to boost ad spending with local media outlets
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today highlighted a sharp decline in advertising expenditure for local media, urging companies to channel more advertising spending to domestic news organisations.
The communications minister said annual advertising expenditure placed directly with local media companies had fallen from about RM4.5 billion previously to around RM2 billion in recent years.
"One of the biggest challenges faced by media practitioners and media companies is advertising expenditure. The amount spent directly with media companies has plunged from about RM4.5 billion a year to only around RM2 billion a year.
"We hope companies, including government-linked companies (GLCs), will take this message seriously and increase media buys with local media companies," he said at the National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 celebration here today.
The event was launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Fahmi said the government remained committed to strengthening the media industry in line with rapid advances in digital technology and artificial intelligence.
He said the Media Innovation Fund, introduced by Anwar during Hawana last year, covered four key areas: media worker training, technology adoption, equipment and facilities and content research and development.
On media welfare, Fahmi said the government had implemented Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) contributions and Employees Provident Fund (EPF) initiatives for freelance media practitioners.
Fahmi also announced that Telekom Malaysia had agreed to become a strategic partner of the fund by contributing RM500,000 through its corporate social responsibility initiative.
"This contribution will help ensure the sustainability of the initiative and enable assistance to be channelled to more media practitioners and former media practitioners in need," he added.
Fahmi said the ministry was also supporting the implementation of Google's Sigma 2.0 project in collaboration with the Malaysian Media Council and the Malaysian Press Institute to enhance media practitioners' knowledge and capabilities in technology and AI.
Meanwhile, after more than three decades of shaping the broadcasting and journalism industry, Datuk Suhaimi Sulaiman received the highest honour of his career when he was awarded the Hawana Award 2026, in recognition of his significant contributions to the development of Malaysia's media landscape.
The award was presented by Anwar.
Wan Syahrina Wan Abdul Rahman, the wife of the late former Bernama Radio chief, Azlan Idris, also received a contribution from Anwar.
Azlan was posthumously named the recipient of the Hawana Special Award 2026.
Meanwhile, at the event, TV3 was announced as the champion, while Bernama emerged as the runner-up in the Hawana-Dewan Bahasa dan Pustaka (DBP) Pantun Festival 2026, held in conjunction with the celebration.
The victory saw TV3 successfully defend the title it won in the competition's inaugural edition last year.
The winning team comprised Mohammad Nor Affiq Norshamsudin, Mohd Safwan Sawi, Azrin Md Isa and Mohamed Hirsham Azmi.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Another feather in the Maha cap: This year's event features foreign participation
Article:
KLANG: The 2026 Malaysia Agriculture, Horticulture and Agrotourism Exhibition (Maha) is set to feature an increased amount of international participation, adding new value to the organisation of the biennial event.
Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said among the countries that have confirmed their participation are Brazil, China, the United States, Japan, South Korea and Hungary.
"InsyaAllah, Maha will run smoothly, with foreign participation. Uzbekistan has said it will attend, along with several other countries.
"This is the first time international participants will be involved because, as I mentioned earlier, food security is interrelated.
"One country alone cannot claim to have solved food issues, as disasters in one country can sometimes require assistance from others," he said at a press conference after launching the Road to Maha 2026 central zone programme here today.
Meanwhile, Agriculture and Food Security Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Isham Ishak said the participation of these countries would allow local exhibitors to enhance their knowledge and gain exposure to the latest agricultural technologies.
He said the participants would also be able to showcase agricultural technologies and services that may not yet be available locally, benefiting both Malaysian farmers and industry players.
"At the same time, it provides opportunities for local participants and visiting delegations to explore the various opportunities offered by our country and by exhibitors," he said.
Isham said business matching sessions would also be held, allowing both local and foreign participants to explore trade opportunities, including buying and selling agricultural products.
Maha 2026 is expected to serve as a strategic platform bringing together modern agricultural technologies, innovation, investment opportunities and industry collaboration.
The event is targeting RM8 billion in memorandum of understanding value and RM58 million in direct sales.
Among the main highlights will be drone technology, farm automation, artificial intelligence based smart systems, agro-tourism attractions and participation from international exhibitors.
To improve public access to information, the Maha 2026 website has also been launched as a one-stop centre for event updates, including exhibitor registration, programme details, activity schedules, exhibition maps and visitor planning tools.
Separately, Mohamad also launched the Monitoring and Intervention Portal for Agri-Food Supply and Market, developed to strengthen the monitoring of the country's food supply and market systems.
The system would allow the government to monitor supply conditions, demand trends and price movements to ensure food supply stability, fair prices for consumers and better returns for agri-food industry players.
At the same event, RM4.4 million in incentives and grants was distributed to 215 agri-food entrepreneurs in the central zone through various departments and agencies under the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar confident Nallini will strengthen public trust in Media Council
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed confidence that the appointment of former Federal Court judge Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan as chairman of the Malaysian Media Council (MMC) would enhance the council's image and strengthen public trust in the body.
He said Nallini's appointment was made after careful consideration of her distinguished judicial career, track record and integrity.
"What is important is that she possesses impeccable credentials and will help provide a new image and confidence for the council. We join others in wishing her success," he said when officiating the National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 highlight event here today.
Also present were Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Among those attending were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah, deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil, Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai, Bernama chief executive officer and Hawana 2026 organising committee chairman Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin, Bernama editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj and Nallini.
Anwar also congratulated Nallini on her appointment as MMC chairman.
Members of the MMC board unanimously endorsed her appointment at a meeting on May 26.
Established under the Malaysian Media Council Act 2025, the MMC is a self-regulatory body tasked with strengthening journalistic standards, managing public complaints involving the media, promoting ethical practices and safeguarding media freedom as a key pillar of democracy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor, Negri polls test Malaysian Media Council election initiative
Article:
BUTTERWORTH: The upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan elections will provide the Malaysian Media Council (MMM) with an opportunity to test and refine a new mechanism aimed at combating fabricated media content and safeguarding public trust in information during election campaigns.
MMM chairman Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan said the close succession of the two polls - Johor on July 11 and Negri Sembilan on Aug 1, offers a practical setting to evaluate and refine the council's newly launched Rapid Response Election Initiative.
"There is a real advantage in that sequence. What we learn in Johor, we can carry directly into Negeri Sembilan, refining the mechanism in real time," she said at the Media Dialogue Session with Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil  here today.
The pilot programme focuses on verifying content falsely attributed to media organisations, including fabricated news graphics, manipulated screenshots and reports carrying forged media logos.
Under the framework, the MMM will act solely as coordinator, while media organisations themselves will verify whether disputed content originated from their platforms.
The Election Commission (EC) will serve as the reference point for election-related matters, while the Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) will assist in disseminating verified information to the public.
"Content Forum Malaysia is our principal partner on digital content, platform engagement, and media literacy, while the Department of Community Communications (J-KOM) and National Information Dissemination Centres (NADI) will carry verified information into communities.
"The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), on the other hand, remains an essential supporting agency where a matter requires regulatory channels, technical assistance, or further action involving platforms," she added.
Nallini said the initiative was not intended to assess the accuracy of political manifestos or campaign claims, but to provide a trusted mechanism for verifying whether content attributed to media organisations was genuine, thereby helping safeguard public confidence in information circulating during election periods.
Explaining how the mechanism would operate, Nallini said a viral graphic falsely bearing the logo of a media organisation and claiming that a candidate has withdrawn could be verified within minutes by the organisation concerned, allowing a correction to be issued before the misinformation gains wider traction.
Similarly, false claims involving election procedures could be referred to the EC for
verification and clarification.
She described the initiative as a practical response to the growing threat posed by synthetic and AI-generated content, which can be rapidly produced and widely disseminated during election campaigns.
Alongside the initiative, the council will run a public awareness campaign carrying the slogan "Who Said It? What's The Source?" to encourage voters to verify information before accepting or sharing it.
"This is not an instruction to stay silent. It is an invitation to think. During an election, citizens have every right to read, share, debate and to take part but healthy participation depends on information that can be trusted.
"So the message is just this: before believing, ask first 'Siapa kata? Sos mana?',"  Nallini said.
Present at the dialogue session, held in conjunction with National Journalists' Day (HAWANA) 2026 celebration, were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and deputy secretary-general (Strategic Communications and Creative Industry) Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil.
Among those in attendance were Bernama chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai, Bernama chief executive officer and HAWANA 2026 working committee chair Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin, Bernama editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj, as well as senior management from local media organisations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'I cannot teach journalism, I hold the ring': Media Council chief defends outsider status
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Media Council (MMC) chair Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan has defended her appointment, saying her distance from both the media industry and politics strengthens the council's independence and impartiality.
She said the Malaysian Media Council Act 2025 was deliberately drafted to prevent politicians, civil servants and lawmakers from holding the chairmanship.
This, she said, was intended to ensure the self-regulatory body earns the trust of both its members and the public.
"The Act that created this council requires that its chair stand apart from politics, from the civil service and from the legislature.
"The law wants this seat occupied by someone outside the contending or competing interests.
"That is not an oversight; it is crafted specifically in the law," she said during an MMC dialogue session on a rapid-response election initiative in Penang today.
Nallini said the council's authority rests not on power, but on public trust and fairness.
A self-regulatory council, she said, exists to set standards, receive complaints and, when necessary, adjudicate those complaints.
Drawing on her 18 years on the bench, Nallini said her judicial background enables her to approach matters impartially and explain decisions transparently.
"It is the principal thing I can bring to this table.
"I cannot teach a journalist how to report; that craft belongs to the editors and reporters in this room, and it is not mine to instruct.
"What I can do is hold the ring — protect the independence of this council, insist on fair process, and endeavour to ensure that when the council speaks, it does so without fear or favour," said the former Federal Court judge.
Reflecting on the council's formative months, Nallini said drafting its constitution was a critical phase to ensure the body is built on the principles of natural justice, proportionality and transparency, with decisions capable of withstanding scrutiny.
"If we get the foundations right — natural justice, proportion, reasons that can be read and tested — the council's standing will follow," she said.
Nallini also underscored the dual nature of press freedom, saying a free media must operate responsibly.
"And a responsible media must, in turn, be protected from pressure, harassment, misuse of its name and manipulation.
"Freedom and responsibility are two halves of the same trust," she said.
Operating independently, she said, the council aims to address key industry gaps involving public recourse, journalistic consistency, digital media oversight and content authenticity through consent-based initiatives rather than punitive enforcement.
To achieve this, the MMC will establish a fair out-of-court dispute resolution mechanism and help elevate standards across the profession.
However, Nallini stressed that such processes must never be weaponised to suppress robust journalism that is essential to a functioning democracy.
"Robust reporting — the kind that discomfits the powerful and asks unwelcome questions — is not a defect to be corrected; it is the very thing a free press exists to do.
"The council will uphold standards, yes.
"But it will be vigilant in ensuring that the upholding of standards is not turned into a means of discouraging the very journalism a democracy most needs," she said.
Meanwhile, ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan elections, the MMC urged newsroom leaders to appoint dedicated verification officers to counter misinformation and disinformation spread online by parties attempting to impersonate legitimate news organisations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negotiations ongoing with Thailand over restrictions on seafood imports, exports
Article:
KLANG: Malaysia is negotiating with Thailand over the restrictions on seafood products being imported and exported between the two countries.
Agriculture and Food Security Ministry secretary-general Datuk Seri Isham Ishak said Malaysia had already received feedback from Thailand regarding the restrictions, particularly on fish and shrimp products.
"The matter is currently at the negotiation stage. Since it has reached the level of the prime ministers, discussions are being conducted intensively to find the best solution.
"We are trying to safeguard our interests, while Thailand is also looking at ways to ensure greater clarity in the export of fish and shrimp products to Malaysia," he told reporters after launching the Road to 2026 Malaysia Agriculture, Horticulture and Agrotourism Exhibition central zone programme here today.
Isham said the government was still assessing and validating the information received before making any decisions.
"We have received the feedback, and what we need to do now is evaluate, validate and audit the information received to ensure it complies with our biosecurity requirements," he said.
Thailand last month imposed restrictions on imports of Malaysian sea bass following concerns over chemical residues detected in the fish.
Malaysia then tightened import controls on
fisheries products
from Thailand, including the enforcement of a requirement for a Certificate of Analysis for siakap (sea bass), as well as a temporary restriction on five shrimp species effective June 1.
The affected species are Penaeus esculentus (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn) and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
In response, Thai Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul on June 2 directed
immediate discussions
with Malaysia over the temporary ban on imports of the five shrimp species, warning that prolonged restrictions could affect shrimp farmers, exporters and the wider seafood supply chain.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Muda names Rashifa for Puteri Wangsa
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU: The Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) has named Puteri Wangsa service centre head Rashifa Aljunied as its candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat in the Johor election.
The announcement was made by Muda president Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, who won the seat in the 2022 Johor election.
"The decision to field Rashifa, 26, was made after in-depth discussions with the party's top leadership and grassroots machinery," she said at a press conference here today.
Amira Aisya said she would not be contesting the seat as she intends to contest a parliamentary seat in the 16th General Election (GE16).
Muda is expected to announce more candidates for the Johor election at an event in Kuala Lumpur tomorrow.
In the 2022 Johor election, Puteri Wangsa was the only seat won by Muda.
Amira Aisya secured victory with a majority of 7,114 votes in a six-cornered contest.
The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the Johor election, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Speaker receives notice on Hamzah's reappointment as opposition leader
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has received a letter from Perikatan Nasional (PN) chief whip Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan notifying the reappointment of Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader.
"Yes, I have," Johari said in a brief reply to the New Straits Times.
Hamzah, who is also the Larut member of parliament, will occupy the opposition leader's seat opposite Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, according to the Dewan Rakyat's latest seating arrangement updated on June 19.
[[nid:1468149]]
Hamzah will sit beside Kemaman member of parliament and PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar.
Samsuri was briefly appointed to fill the vacancy following Hamzah's resignation as opposition leader on April 12 after he was sacked by Bersatu on Feb 13.
However,
Takiyuddin submitted a notification on June 18 informing the Speaker of Hamzah's reinstatement
following a meeting involving 62 MPs a day earlier.
Indera Mahkota member of parliament Datuk Seri Saifuddin Abdullah told the New Straits Times that Hamzah's reappointment was supported by all 43 Pas MPs and 19 MPs aligned with Hamzah's "Reset" movement, including himself.
The 19 Reset MPs include Datuk Seri Ronald Kiandee (Beluran), Zakri Hassan (Kangar), Datuk Dr Ku Abd Rahman Ku Ismail (Kubang Pasu), Roslan Hashim (Kulim Bandar Baharu), Datuk Khlir Mohd Nor (Ketereh), Datuk Seri Ikmal Hisham Abdul Aziz (Tanah Merah) and Wan Ahmad Fayhsal Wan Ahmad Kamal (Machang).
Also part of the movement are Datuk Rosol Wahid (Hulu Terengganu), Datuk Wan Saiful Wan Jan (Tasek Gelugor), Fathul Huzir Ayob (Gerik), Azahari Hasan (Padang Rengas), Nordin Ahmad Ismail (Lumut), Datuk Abdul Khalib Abdullah (Rompin), Kalam Salan (Sabak Bernam), Datuk Muslimin Yahaya (Sungai Besar), Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin (Masjid Tanah) and Muhammad Islahuddin Abas (Mersing).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi seeks stronger media ties with Timor-Leste through Bernama, RTM
Article:
Fahmi proposes expanded cooperation between Malaysian and Timor-Leste media agencies, including journalist training programmes through IPPTAR. –
BUTTERWORTH: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil is seeking to strengthen media cooperation with Timor-Leste through agencies under his ministry, including the Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) and Radio Televisyen Malaysia (RTM).
The proposal was raised during a meeting with a Timor-Leste delegation led by the country's Secretary of State for Social Communication, Expedito Loro Dias Ximenes. Also present were Agencia Noticiosa de Timor-Leste (Tatoli) president Noemio Mateus Soares Falcao and deputy president Madalena DE JCP Carlos.
"My counterpart and I hope to further enhance cooperation between Bernama and RTM and their counterparts in Timor-Leste.
"I have also proposed that the Tun Abdul Razak Broadcasting and Information Institute (Ipptar) host training sessions for journalists from Timor-Leste," he told reporters after the meeting.
During the discussion, Fahmi and Ximenes exchanged views on Timor-Leste's standing in the World Press Freedom Index published by Reporters Without Borders (RSF), in which the country ranks highest in Southeast Asia and 30th globally.
"I also shared my views on how Timor-Leste has managed to achieve such a high ranking in the RSF World Press Freedom Index. Timor-Leste is ranked first in Southeast Asia, while Malaysia is second," he said.
Fahmi said the Madani Government remained committed to strengthening policies, initiatives and efforts aimed at improving Malaysia's position in the index.
"Although Malaysia's standing has improved significantly over the past three to four years under the MADANI Government compared with previous administrations, we believe there is still room to enhance our efforts, policies and initiatives to climb higher in the RSF World Press Freedom Index," he said.
Also present at the meeting were Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah, deputy secretary-general Datuk Bahria Mohd Tamil, Bernama chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai, Bernama chief executive officer Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin, Broadcasting Department director-general Ashwad Ismail and Bernama editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj.
The Timor-Leste delegation is in Malaysia for the Hawana 2026 main event, which is being held at the Picca Convention Centre @ Butterworth Arena today.
The sixth edition of Hawana has brought together about 1,000 local and foreign media practitioners under the theme "Media Integrity Strengthens Credibility", with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim scheduled to officiate the event at 3pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Demolition continues at Rohingya-linked structures in Hulu Langat
Article:
HULU LANGAT: Demolition of residential structures linked to the Rohingya community at Kampung Baru Sungai Makau, Sungai Tekali, is continuing in stages by the landowner.
A Harian Metro visit at 11am yesterday found several homes built on river reserve land behind a four-storey flat had been demolished.
Clean-up works and removal of debris, including metal and concrete, are being carried out by about 10 workers.
Another house is being demolished using an excavator before work shifts to the four-storey structure.
The 38-year-old son of the landowner, who declined to be named, said the demolition was nearly 50 per cent complete.
He said the work was being done in phases and funded by the family.
"Structures built on river reserve and government land have been demolished, while the remaining structures are on our family land.
"The remaining sections will continue until completion," he told Harian Metro.
He said the family had cooperated with authorities conducting near-daily inspections.
"We also provide regular updates to the relevant authorities," he said.
He apologised to those affected and urged time to complete the process.
"We are doing our best to resolve this matter and hope the public will give us space.
"We are committed to complying with the authorities' instructions," he said.
Dusun Tua assemblyman Datuk Johan Abd Aziz said authorities were allowing voluntary demolition before further action.
"Next week, we will inspect the progress," he said.
Authorities and agencies are monitoring the operation to ensure it proceeds smoothly.
Previously, it was reported an alleged Rohingya community had built structures resembling a four-storey flat in Sungai Tekali.
The issue emerged after a viral video by radio presenter and actor Azad Jasmin, who recorded the site showing multi-storey structures, zinc-roofed corridors, unfinished brick walls, and exposed utilities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Soldier driving to hometown for his wedding told fiancee died in road crash
Article:
TEMERLOH: A soldier was on his way to his hometown for his wedding on June 26 when he received news that his 23-year-old fiancee had been killed in a road crash near Jengka 19 here yesterday.
Mohamad Alif Iskandar Azhar, 24, was scheduled to tie the knot with Dinie Hanis Noor Azlan Efenddy, 23. The couple had been together since their school days.
Sadly, she and her sister were killed in the crash.
Alif Iskandar said he was caught in traffic at the Gombak toll plaza when his sibling called to inform him that Dinie Hanis had been involved in an accident.
"Several minutes later, I was informed that she had died. I had just begun my leave for the wedding and was travelling back to Jengka.
"We had completed all the wedding preparations, and both families were only waiting for the big day. We were very close, and Dinie Hanis was looking forward to the wedding," he said today.
The 7.25pm crash at Km24 Jalan Temerloh–Jerantut also claimed the life of Dinie Hanis' younger sister, Dinie Nasuha, 19, after the multi-purpose vehicle they were travelling in from Bandar Jengka towards Temerloh
collided head-on
with a car while overtaking a motorcycle.
Alif Iskandar said his fiancee, who was friendly and hardworking, had taken on the responsibility of helping her family earn a living after her father passed away a few years ago.
"She and her siblings worked hard. They were involved in business activities to support their mother," he said.
He said that even during their final conversation a few days before the crash, they were still discussing wedding preparations and matters that needed to be settled.
"She was happy because we had been in a relationship since school days and were finally going to begin life as husband and wife. She was excited to invite relatives and friends to our wedding," he said.
Alif Iskandar said the moments they shared together, along with her laughter, would be what he would miss the most.
He led the funeral prayer for Dinie Hanis before she was buried at the Sungai Gau Muslim cemetery here.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar hails Petronas gas deal in Turkmenistan
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Turkmenistan's decision to award two major gas fields to Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) marked a milestone for Malaysia's energy security.
Anwar said the agreement, announced during his visit to Turkmenistan, would strengthen Malaysia's position in the global energy sector while supporting long-term supply commitments to key international markets.
He said the development was significant as it enhanced Malaysia's capacity to manage large-scale energy trade and meet growing demand from major economies.
"I returned feeling relieved and grateful because this is part of our continuing historical achievement.
"Secondly, it strengthens our trade management capabilities in the energy sector on a very large scale, enabling us to meet demand from Japan, Korea, China and other countries, including Australia, which are now trading with us on a long-term basis.
"I am thankful. This is great news," he said at the launch of National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 here today.
Present were Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Also present was Media Prima Bhd News and Current Affairs deputy managing editor and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim.
Anwar said the gas field award reflected international confidence in Petronas' capabilities, built on three decades of experience and a strong track record in the energy sector.
He said Turkmenistan's leadership had recognised Malaysia's approach of working as a development partner rather than merely pursuing commercial interests.
The prime minister said the project would bring long-term benefits to Malaysia while strengthening bilateral ties and expanding the country's role in global energy markets.
"I hope our friends in Johor, Negeri Sembilan, Sabah and across the country will hear this good news and see how Malaysia is managing its affairs and building strong relationships with other nations," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gleneagles Johor leads Malaysia in advanced patient recovery care
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: Gleneagles Hospital Johor has become the first hospital in the country to achieve certification for the Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS®) Implementation Programme (EIP) Total Knee Arthroplasty Protocol.
The ERAS® programme is an internationally recognised, evidence-based framework designed to improve surgical outcomes by reducing recovery time, minimising complications, and enhancing the overall patient experience before, during, and after surgery.
The programme is a structured training and certification initiative developed to support healthcare teams in achieving consistent, high-quality adherence to ERAS® protocols across surgical disciplines. It is delivered in collaboration with the global clinic experts from the ERAS® Society.
At Gleneagles Hospital Johor, the implementation was led by Dr Shahridan Mohd Fathil and coordinator, Senior Staff Nurse Ernizah Hamzah.
The initiative received strong support from Gleneagles Hospital Johor deputy chief executive officer Sipika Singh and IHH Healthcare Malaysia chief executive officer Dr Kamal Amzan.
Hospital officials described the certification as a reflection of sustained efforts to elevate patient-centred care beyond conventional surgical treatment, focusing instead on improving the entire perioperative journey.
The ERAS® model places strong emphasis on preparation, early mobilisation, and coordinated multidisciplinary care, which together help patients recover more efficiently and safely.
According to the hospital, patients undergoing procedures such as Total Knee Arthroplasty under Orthopaedics, Anterior Resection under Colorectal Surgery, and selected gynaecological surgeries are now experiencing fast recovery timelines, reduced side effects from anaesthesia, and earlier return to daily activities — often within as little as one day post-surgery for suitable cases.
Surgeons, anaesthesiologists, nurses, physiotherapists, and dietitians work in close coordination throughout the entire surgical journey to ensure continuity of care and optimal patient outcomes.
PRE-SURGERY PHASE
In the preoperative stage, patients are actively prepared for surgery through education, physical conditioning, and nutritional guidance.
The multidisciplinary team assesses each patient's condition to optimise health status before surgery.
Physiotherapists provide mobility and breathing exercises, while dietitians ensure nutritional readiness to support healing and reduce surgical risks. This early preparation is designed to reduce complications and enhance resilience during recovery.
DURING SURGERY
During surgical procedures, the hospital employs advanced anaesthesia techniques aimed at reducing discomfort and minimising common postoperative side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and drowsiness.
[[nid:1468124]]
Evidence-based surgical practices, including careful fluid management and temperature control, are also implemented to ensure patient stability and safety.
SURGERY
Following surgery, patients are encouraged to begin early mob
ilisation, often within four hours of the procedure when clinically appropriate.
This early movement is a key principle of ERAS®, helping to reduce the risk of complications such as blood clots and muscle deconditioning while accelerating recovery.
Continuous physiotherapy support and clinical monitoring ensure that patients regain mobility safely and progressively, enabling a faster return to normal activities.
Patients also benefit from improved post-operative comfort, reduced reliance on strong analgesics, and a more positive overall recovery experience.
The hospital reports that this structured approach has significantly improved both clinical outcomes and patient satisfaction.
Sipika emphasised the importance of the achievement in strengthening the hospital's role in advancing healthcare standards in Malaysia.
"At Gleneagles Hospital Johor, our patients' comfort, recovery, and overall well-being remain at the heart of everything we do. This milestone further differentiates us as a hospital that continuously pioneers healthcare excellence and innovation."
The certification process was further strengthened through coaching and supervision in collaboration with a leading hospital in Singapore, ensuring that implementation at Gleneagles Hospital Johor aligned with international best practices and global clinical standards.
The hospital has also expanded ERAS® practices across its Colorectal Surgery and Obstetrics & Gynaecology services, reinforcing its position as a leader in perioperative care innovation within the private healthcare sector.
The hospital's ongoing expansion of ERAS® principles into additional specialities signals a long-term commitment to safe procedures, fast recovery, and enhanced patient-centred care across the region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Anwar announces RM1mil boost for journalists' welfare fund at Hawana 2026
Article:
PERMATANG PAUH: The government has allocated RM1 million to Tabung Kasih@Hawana 2026 and will continue supporting initiatives aimed at transforming the media industry, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Announcing the allocation today, Anwar said the contribution reflected the government's appreciation for media practitioners and its commitment to their welfare.
"I was informed that earlier today the fund received contributions totalling RM2.6 million. In view of this spirit of compassion and care, Tabung Kasih@Hawana 2026 will receive an allocation of RM1 million," he said at the launch of National Journalists' Day (Hawana) 2026 here today.
Present were Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow and Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil.
Also present was Media Prima Bhd News and Current Affairs deputy managing editor and New Straits Times group editor Farrah Naz Karim.
Anwar also announced that the government would continue the Media Innovation Fund to help media organisations strengthen innovation efforts and accelerate digital transformation.
He said the government had previously allocated RM30 million for the fund, which was announced last year.
According to Anwar, 72 media companies have received a total of RM24.57 million under the first five phases of the Media Innovation Fund.
"We provide the allocation and, once the fund is utilised, we will certainly add more so that it will not face any shortage.
"This is good news for all of you," he added.
The prime minister stressed the importance of responsible and effective dissemination of information, saying media practitioners play a critical role in ensuring the public receives accurate information.
He expressed hope that the media would continue to uphold professionalism and ethical standards in carrying out their responsibilities.
"Media practitioners must continue to uphold ethics and integrity in reporting as technological advances reshape the information landscape," he said, adding that the media's responsibility extended beyond embracing digital transformation.
Drawing lessons from the trial of ancient Greek philosopher Socrates, Anwar said the challenge facing modern media was not merely the dissemination of information but ensuring it was conveyed truthfully and responsibly.
"In the clash and contest of ideas, there must be ethics in conveying the truth.
"People often focus on digitalisation, energy transition and artificial intelligence, but forget that in all these new frameworks, the aspects of ethics and values cannot be neglected.
"It is ethics and values that determine whether what is conveyed is true or false, right or wrong," he said.
Anwar said the role of media practitioners had become even more critical in an era where information could be transmitted instantly and influence public opinion on a massive scale.
He also said freedom of expression remained important, but the dissemination of information must be guided by responsibility and a commitment to facts.
"The way information is communicated will determine how people make judgments and decisions.
"This is a major challenge for media practitioners and journalists," he said.
Anwar added that Malaysia's efforts to build a more stable political system and pursue sustainable development depended significantly on the role played by the media.
"A more stable system and politics that focus on development, while emphasising values and ethics, depend greatly on the attitude and role of media practitioners and journalists," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Father mourns doctor son, granddaughter killed in Pekan crash
Article:
PEKAN: The father of a doctor killed in a road crash that also killed his two-year-old granddaughter said he was devastated after learning of their deaths.
Mohd Atan Daud, 67, said his son, Dr Mohd Zekry Zuhairy Mohd Atan, 33, head of the forensic unit at Segamat Hospital, was travelling with his wife, daughter and in-laws from Segamat, Johor, to Kuantan in a Honda Civic.
"They were on their way to visit Zekry's sister-in-law, who is a medical officer at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA).
"About 7pm, I received a call informing me that my son had been involved in an accident and was sent to Pekan Hospital.
"I assumed it was not serious. I then travelled with my wife and daughter to Pekan Hospital. During the journey, my daughter contacted the hospital, and we were shocked to learn that my son and granddaughter had died," he said when met at the Pekan Hospital forensic unit.
The
6pm crash killed Zekry Zuhairy and his daughter, Dhea Sofia
, while his 33-year-old wife, also a doctor, remains in critical condition at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA).
Zekry's 80-year-old father-in-law is being treated for serious injuries at HTAA, while his 72-year-old mother-in-law is warded with bruises at Pekan Hospital.
Mohd Atan said he last saw his son and granddaughter last week when they visited him in Segamat.
"My son told me he would take me for my appointment at Sultanah Aminah Hospital in Johor Baru this coming week for follow-up treatment for my liver cancer.
"Since I was diagnosed with liver cancer five years ago, Zekry would always take me for my hospital appointments," he said.
On the accident, Atan said he was told a pick-up truck lost control and crashed into his son's car and another vehicle.
"I leave it to the police to investigate and take action if my family members were killed due to negligence," he said.
Atan also said he was informed that his daughter-in-law suffered facial, oral and abdominal injuries.
The remains of Zekry Zuhairy and Dhea Sofia were claimed by family members at about 11am today and will be buried at the Muadzam Shah Muslim burial ground in Rompin.
The crash yesterday also killed Toyota Alphard driver Azaharuddin Mohd Ali, 46, while his wife and son were injured.
His remains were claimed by family members and buried in Raub.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rahmah welfare aid: STR Sara budget increased to RM15bil
Article:
LUNDU: A total of nine million recipients nationwide are benefiting from the Rahmah Cash Aid (STR) and Rahmah Basic Aid (Sara) programmes, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said.
He said the government has allocated RM15 billion this year for the two aid initiatives, up from RM10 billion in 2024.
For Sarawak, he said RM1.3 billion has been allocated for nearly 900,000 STR and Sara recipients in the state.
He added that the Finance Ministry has set up 20 locations in Sarawak and 17 in Sabah to make it easier for STR recipients without bank accounts to claim their aid in cash.
Amir Hamzah said the initiative was introduced to ensure government assistance reaches target groups directly, including rural residents and senior citizens who face difficulties accessing banking services.
"So far, the programme has been carried out at 15 locations in Sarawak, with five more locations to follow next week.
"STR recipients without bank accounts can also claim their cash assistance at any Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) branch nationwide," he said after the STR Cash Out and Mobile Community Services Mini-Programme (Mini-MCS).
He said eligible recipients also receive Sara, a monthly assistance programme that can be used to purchase daily essentials at premises registered as Sara Retail Partners.
In a related development, Amir Hamzah said Sarawak had recorded encouraging results in the implementation of Sara, with 99 per cent of recipients using their MyKad to make purchases at registered premises.
"This proves that the MyKad-based Sara mechanism is simple, safe and effective, especially in rural areas," he said.
He added that Sara recipients receive between RM600 and RM2,400 annually, depending on their eligibility category, with total assistance reaching up to RM2,500 when including the one-off Sara Penghargaan payout.
As of June 16, the number of Sara Retail Partners nationwide had increased to 13,491 premises, compared with just 700 in 2024, he said.
On the Mini-MCS programme, Amir Hamzah said the initiative, which brings together 11 government agencies, would be expanded to more locations to enable the public to access government assistance and services more quickly and effectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jamal settles RM66,600 debt to Teresa Kok, avoids auction of belongings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Just three days before a public auction of his belongings was set to take place, Sungai Besar Umno chief Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos made a last-minute move to settle a judgment debt owed to DAP Teresa Kok.
This averts the Monday sale of 14 household items seized from his residence.
Jamal tendered a banker's cheque amounting to RM66,600 at 5pm yesterday to fully satisfy the outstanding legal costs awarded to Kok, the Seputeh member of parliament.
The auction was part of execution proceedings to recover the unpaid sum through the sale of items seized from Jamal's home.
In a statement issued today, Kok's lawyer, Datuk S.N. Nair, confirmed receipt of the payment and announced that the auction had been called off for the time being.
"In view of the payment received, the public auction previously scheduled for June 22 has been called off and postponed pending clearance of the banker's cheque.
"Upon successful clearance of the banker's cheque, the judgment debt shall be deemed fully satisfied and the auction proceedings will be cancelled accordingly.
"The sum paid is costs and is inclusive of interest and allocator," he said.
Among the 14 household items are a massage chair, a 10-seater dining table, three units of two seater sofa, air conditioning units and television. (https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/06/1461706/auction-set-seized-jamal-assets-mp-teresa-kok-costs-dispute)
Last week, Nair had said the items would be auctioned off after the High Court disposed of two interpleader claims before senior assistant registrar Qasiratul Jannah Usmani Othman.
He had said one claimant, Mohd Razali Kamin, who said he owned several seized items, filed a notice of discontinuance and was ordered to pay RM2,000 in costs.
Meanwhile, the interpleader claim by Jamal's son, Muhammad Amin Daniel Jamal, was dismissed with costs of RM8,000.
On Jan 28, a court assistant registrar ordered a forced entry at Jamal's residence in Ampang Jaya, resulting in the seizure of 14 household items to recover the outstanding sum.
The action stemmed from a final High Court judgment obtained by Kok against Jamal in a defamation suit in 2022.
On July 26, 2022, Kok won the suit over Jamal's defamatory remarks concerning the alleged misuse of Yayasan Warisan Anak Selangor funds during a press conference on March 8, 2017.
She had filed the suit on April 6, 2017.
Judicial commissioner Datuk Mohd Arief Emran Arifin ordered Jamal to pay RM300,000 in general, aggravated and exemplary damages, as well as RM50,000 in costs.
On March 29, 2023, the Court of Appeal granted Jamal a conditional stay, requiring him to deposit RM300,000 in damages into Kok's solicitors' account, which he did.
A separate Court of Appeal panel upheld the High Court ruling on March 27, 2024.
The Federal Court subsequently dismissed Jamal's application for leave to appeal on July 30, 2024.
Kok obtained the writ of seizure and sale after Jamal failed to pay the RM50,000 in legal costs.
Nair said Jamal had settled the damages award, but the legal costs remained unpaid, prompting enforcement action at his residence.
A previous attempt to execute the order on Oct 9, 2024, failed because no one was at home.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH under no pressure to name Johor election 'poster boy'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan will not be pressured into naming a menteri besar candidate just to satisfy its rivals ahead of the 16th Johor election.
Amanah Youth executive committee member Nur Hafiz Roslan said the coalition is instead prioritising the selection of a capable leader of high integrity who can unite the state's multiracial population.
"We are more focused on reaching a collective agreement in a harmonious atmosphere. What is more important is that the chosen menteri besar candidate must be capable, possess commendable traits, and be polite and prudent.
"It must be someone who consistently strives to unite people of various races and backgrounds, and advocates for unity in Johor, not the other way around," he said in a statement.
He was responding to Johor Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who had questioned who PH's menteri besar candidate would be should the coalition form the state government after the upcoming polls.
Nur Hafiz said PH, comprising Amanah, PKR and DAP, practises democracy and mutual respect among its component parties, devoid of the "big brother" concept seen in other political pacts.
He said Johor needs a new generation of leaders who understand religious values, are professional, and capable of driving a unity agenda for the future of the state and its people.
He added that the person chosen as menteri besar must also prioritise the people's voices rather than merely pushing party agendas or resorting to political gimmicks.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Filipino man arrested with 10 live pangolins, elephant tusk in Sabah raid
Article:
KINABATANGAN: A 27-year-old Filipino man has been arrested after he was found keeping 10 live pangolins at a plantation in Kampung Paris 3 yesterday.
The suspect was detained during Op Bersepadu Khazanah by the Sabah Wildlife Department (SWD) and the General Operations Force Battalion 15 at the entrance of The Yang Plantation at about 8.30pm.
During the operation, authorities also found two plastic packages containing an estimated 9kg of pangolin scales and a tusk believed to be from an elephant.
SWD director Mohd Soffian Abu Bakar said the man was arrested for illegal possession of protected wildlife and wildlife parts.
"All the wildlife specimens and parts were seized, and the suspect has been detained to assist investigations under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.
"We will continue to strengthen strategic cooperation with other enforcement agencies to curb illegal wildlife hunting activities that threaten Sabah's biodiversity treasures," he said.
Under the Sabah Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997, offences involving totally protected wildlife carry penalties of up to RM250,000 in fines, five years' jail, or both.
Mohd Soffian also urged the public to report any wildlife-related offences immediately to the authorities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Winning election remains priority before naming Johor MB, says Loke
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PH's candidate for Johor Menteri Besar, should the coalition win the Johor state election, will be decided later by the coalition's top leadership council.
PH deputy chairman Anthony Loke said the coalition's immediate priority is securing victories for its candidates in the election.
"Therefore, the matter of the Johor Menteri Besar will be discussed at the highest level of PH. At this stage, there has been no announcement," he said.
Loke, who is also DAP secretary-general, said this at a press conference after the announcement of PH candidates for the Johor Jaya (N.42) and Tiram (N.40) state seats at Pusat Komuniti Harapan in Plentong today.
At the event, Nor Zulaila Abd Ghani, 38, and Lee Wern Yiing, 30, were announced as PH candidates for the Johor Jaya and Tiram state seats, respectively.
Also present were Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching and deputy chairman Wong Shu Qi.
Earlier, Loke was asked who would serve as PH's poster boy and Menteri Besar candidate if the coalition won the Johor state election.
"For DAP, every constituency we contest will have its own poster boy or poster girl, based on the party's candidate contesting there.
"DAP's target is to win and retain the seats it previously secured, including Ulu Tiram," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Portugal's unofficial exorcism fever worries Church
Article:
FATIMA: A woman closes her eyes as a young man dressed as a priest places both hands on her forehead. Moments later, she falls to the floor in front of dozens of worshippers.
The scene unfolds not in a church but in the basement of a small shopping centre a short walk from the vast esplanade of Portugal's Fatima shrine, one of the Catholic Church's most visited pilgrimage sites.
The gathering was part of a series of monthly so-called "exorcism retreats" held in Fatima that have raised concerns among Catholic authorities, who say they operate outside official Church oversight by self-styled religious figures and risk exploiting vulnerable believers.
On a recent Saturday, more than 100 people waited for the moment when Francisco Marques, 27, would start laying his hands on participants one by one.
Wearing a black cassock and a Roman collar, Marques cuts the figure of a traditional Catholic cleric.
But neither the Vatican nor local Church authorities recognise his ministry, which was founded in 2006.
Marques placed both hands on their foreheads, concentrating silently.
Some participants — mostly women — immediately fall backwards into the arms of attendants standing ready behind them before being lowered onto mats spread across the floor.
"You get a great feeling of peace. It's a liberation. I leave here cleansed, with a lighter soul," Lurdes Ramisio, a 56-year-old nurse, told AFP at the retreat.
"I feel he has a power. His hands transmit a power to me," she added.
'DRIVING OUT DEMONS'
The sessions are organised by Marques and members of his family with the support of Salvatore Micalef, an Italian who presents himself as his bishop.
"When I met Francisco, I saw above all that he had the gift of driving out demons," Micalef told AFP. "I therefore issued a decree authorising him to be an exorcist."
Exorcism is the ancient practice of driving out demons or evil spirits from a person or place they are thought to possess.
It is practised by some Roman Catholics but treated with deep scepticism by others.
[[nid:1468077]]
While exorcism is an officially recognised practice within Catholicism, it is governed by strict rules and reserved for specially authorised priests.
Church officials say cases of alleged demonic possession are approached with caution and often involve consultation with medical experts to ensure people with illnesses such as epilepsy do not miss out on medical treatment if their symptoms are wrongly ascribed to the supernatural.
Bishop Jose Ornelas of Leiria-Fatima told AFP the Church treats the subject "with great prudence" to avoid creating the impression that priests are "gurus" with special powers over demons.
In 2023, his diocese warned against what it called "suspicious retreats" organised by "a supposed seminarian friend of the pope."
HOLY WATER AND 'EXORCISED' SALT
Ornelas said that while the Church lacks the legal authority to prohibit such gatherings, it has the responsibility to denounce "abuse" by those who "exploit" people's suffering for their own benefit.
Marques says he is the victim of a campaign against him and has filed a defamation complaint against Church authorities.
"We have been slandered. We have been called false priests, false bishops, fraudsters. We must defend our dignity," he said.
Beyond the monthly retreats in Fatima, Marques celebrates services every Sunday in a private chapel at his residence in a village some 100 kilometres (60 miles) north of the shrine town.
He insists participation in the exorcism sessions is free of charge, though he acknowledges that donations from believers help finance the activities.
Visitors are also encouraged to purchase items including "exorcised" salt, holy water and anointing oils prepared by Marques.
The items are displayed on a table alongside business cards featuring a photo of him with Pope Francis, a telephone number and a bank account number for contributions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Toddler injured as wild boar rams into pram in Poland
Article:
WARSAW: A wild boar collided with a pram in the southern Polish city of Kraków, injuring a 13-month-old girl, German Press Agency (dpa) reported, citing police.
The toddler, who had been lying in the pram, had to be taken to hospital with head injuries, the PAP news agency reported on Friday, citing the police. Her injuries are not life-threatening.
Initial media reports had said an aggressive wild boar had attacked the pram without warning on Thursday on a footpath in the city's Kliny district.
A crisis team convened by Kraków city authorities met on Friday to discuss possible safety measures. During the meeting, it emerged that the wild boar had not rammed the pram unprovoked.
Apparently, a herd of wild boars had been startled by a dog and had fled onto the footpath, where they happened to cross paths with a mother pushing a pram. – BERNAMA-dpa
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain, thunderstorms expected in three states and Labuan until 3pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit Johor, Sarawak, Sabah and Labuan until 3pm today.
The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement today, said the severe weather forecast involves Kluang, Mersing, Pontian, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru.
Similar warnings have been issued for Sarikei (Julau), Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Bintulu and Sebauh), Miri, and Limbang (Limbang) in Sarawak.
In Sabah, similar conditions are expected to hit the interiors (Kuala Penyu and Beaufort), the West Coast (Papar), Sandakan (Beluran) and Kudat (Pitas). – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Air force invites public to run on Kuantan air base runway tomorrow
Article:
KUANTAN: Members of the public are invited to experience the rare thrill of running on an active runway at the air force open day celebration here tomorrow (June 21).
The air force runway run, held within an operational air base, promises participants an unforgettable experience as they race along a runway usually reserved for aircraft, with air force assets displayed in the background.
Air force chief General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris said that although registration for about 3,000 participants has been closed, those who did not sign up are still welcome to join the event tomorrow.
"Participants who signed up earlier for the race will receive a special edition T-shirt. However, those who did not register can join in suitable attire. They can either run or walk on the runway.
"The unique event offers participants the rare opportunity to race on the airport tarmac, which is classified as a restricted zone. The runway run is among the highlights of this year's anniversary celebration," he told reporters after opening the air force's two-day 68th anniversary programme at the Kuantan air base.
He said participants will also have the opportunity to get close to the nation's air force assets, which will be displayed along the runway, providing a scenic backdrop.
Norazlan said the relatively uncongested airspace at the base makes it suitable for the running event, which was last held more than a decade ago.
The running event tomorrow features two categories: 6km and 12km.
Meanwhile, he said the annual anniversary programme also offers the public an opportunity to understand the air force's role in safeguarding the nation's airspace while strengthening ties between the military and the community.
"The public can seek clarification and gain insights into the air force's direction and capabilities. We can share information on our roles, future assets and job opportunities.
"While matters involving national security remain confidential, we are committed to sharing information that helps Malaysians better appreciate the air force's contribution," he said.
Earlier today, thousands of visitors converged on the air base to get up close with military assets and witness aerial performances.
Visitor Muhd Al Muimin Khairul Amir, 24, from Arau, Perlis, said he was in Kuantan visiting a relative and decided to drop by the open day.
"This is my first time at the event. The low-flying helicopters and roaring fighter jets drew a lot of excitement," he said after having a picture taken with Norazlan.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lebanon state media says five dead in fresh Israeli strikes on south
Article:
BEIRUT: Lebanese official media said fresh Israeli strikes on the country's south on Saturday killed five people, despite a new ceasefire between Israel and the Iran-backed Hizbollah group announced a day earlier.
The state-run National News Agency (NNA) reported Israeli airstrikes on more than a dozen south Lebanon locations after midnight and into Saturday morning, many in and around the Nabatieh area.
It also reported Israeli artillery shelling on Nabatieh city and its outskirts, a region where fighting has been focused in recent days.
The NNA said three people were killed in airstrikes on the town of Arab Salim, while one person was killed in Deir Zahrani, and another after "an enemy drone launched a strike on a motorbike" at the entrance of the town of Dweir.
On Friday, a US official told AFP an immediate truce between Israel and Hizbollah had been brokered by US and Qatari mediators following talks with Israel and Iran. A Gulf diplomat confirmed the ceasefire.
Israel's ambassador to the US said his country would commit to the ceasefire if Hizbollah respected it.
Previous truce announcements have done little to stop attacks from either side.
The announcement came as Lebanon's health ministry said Israeli airstrikes and bombardment on the country's south and east killed 47 people on Friday, the worst violence since Washington and Teheran this week sealed a deal to halt the wider Middle East war.
That agreement was supposed to also halt fighting between Israel and Hizbollah in Lebanon.
Israel's military on Friday said four of its soldiers were killed, and reported more than 150 strikes on Lebanon, killing "dozens of Hizbollah fighters".
Also on Friday, Lebanese President Joseph Aoun told US Secretary of State Marco Rubio that a comprehensive ceasefire was needed in order for talks with Israel to progress.
Under US pressure, Lebanon in April began direct talks with Israel in Washington aimed at ending the hostilities and separating the Israel-Hizbollah conflict from the regional war.
A fifth round of talks is due to begin on Tuesday, according to the State Department.
US officials, including President Donald Trump, have expressed frustration at Israel's campaign in Lebanon.
But Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Friday reiterated that Israeli troops would stay in south Lebanon "as long as necessary."
Hizbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war in early March with rocket fire at Israel to avenge the killing of Iran's supreme leader in US-Israeli strikes.
Israel responded with a massive campaign of airstrikes and a ground invasion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman killed, 1,700 evacuated in beach hotel fire in Dominican Republic
Article:
SANTO DOMINGO: A woman was killed and nearly 1,700 tourists were evacuated due to a large fire at a hotel in the Dominican beach resort of Bayahibe, local authorities said on Friday.
Francesca Valentino, a 46-year-old Italian woman, was killed in the fire at the Viva Wyndham Dominicus Beach Hotel, the DAEH emergency service said in a statement.
Three people were taken to medical facilities and six others were treated on site, DAEH said. Those affected included guests, visitors and emergency responders.
Videos shared by local media showed dark clouds of smoke billowing out above the Caribbean coastline, as flames engulfed the resort's straw roof.
"Preliminary observations indicate that the fire spread rapidly due to the flammable nature of parts of the roof structures made of palm, as well as wind conditions," the country's Emergency Operations Center (COE) said.
Wyndham Hotels and Resorts did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The fire was brought under control but its causes were still being investigated, the operations centre said. Guests were moved to nearby hotels. The Viva Wyndham Dominicus Palace, part of the same chain, did not sustain any damage.
"Tourist activities in Bayahibe and the surrounding area remain unaffected and continue to take place safely and as normal," COE added.
Famous for its clear water and white-sand beaches, the Dominican Republic is the Caribbean's top tourist destination, receiving some 5.6 million visitors in the first five months of this year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MOH says advanced specialist training selection is transparent, merit-based
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 20 — The Health Ministry (MOH) stressed today that the selection process for its Advanced Specialist Training Programme (Offer C) is conducted in a structured, transparent and merit-based manner.
The ministry said the selection process involves screening against general eligibility requirements, professional assessments and technical evaluations by the respective specialty disciplines before recommendations are endorsed by the MOH Advanced Specialist Training Programme Steering Committee.
“For the 2026/2027 intake, a total of 672 applications were received involving Medical Subspecialty Programmes, Dental Subspecialty Programmes, Dental Areas of Special Interest (AOSI), Public Health and Family Health.
“Of the total, MOH allocated 400 training slots, and to date, 307 candidates have been offered places in the programme after meeting the general requirements, specialty-specific criteria and professional assessment standards,” it said in a statement today.
It also clarified that requirements relating to the Annual Performance Appraisal Report (LNPT) were not imposed unilaterally by the ministry or its Training Management Division (BPL), but are subject to policies established by the Public Service Department (JPA).
The ministry said that following discussions with JPA, performance assessments conducted during the Supervised Work Experience (SWE) period for specialist medical officers can now be considered in addition to the two years of post-gazettement performance evaluations required for applications to the Advanced Specialist Training Programme.
Commenting on appeals involving 123 applicants, MOH said a cross-review by the BPL and the Medical Development Division (BPP) found that the group did not comprise a single uniform category.
“Of the 123 names submitted, only 20 individuals were identified as being among the 50 candidates currently under review following JPA’s decision dated June 19, 2025.
“Of these, only eight met JPA’s latest requirements to be considered through the inclusion of performance assessments obtained during the SWE period. The remaining 115 applicants were found not to have met the general requirements and specialty-specific criteria set by their respective disciplines,” the statement said.
As such, MOH rejected claims that all 123 applicants were eligible but were denied placement solely because of issues related to LNPT requirements.
At the same time, the ministry acknowledged that differences exist in the implementation of training between Master’s Programmes and Parallel Pathway Programmes, which have evolved according to prevailing policies and implementation methods.
According to MOH, officers undergoing the Parallel Pathway Programme generally remain in their substantive positions and continue serving at MOH healthcare facilities, enabling them to receive LNPT evaluations throughout their training period.
“In contrast, participants in Master’s Programmes under the Full-Pay Study Leave with Federal Training Award (HLP) scheme generally do not receive LNPT assessments as they are on study leave and are subject to different academic and professional evaluation mechanisms,” it said.
The ministry also noted that some officers pursuing training through the Parallel Pathway Programme are placed in Training Reserve Posts (JSL) or are awaiting placement in such positions, resulting in performance evaluations not being implemented uniformly across all facilities and responsibility centres.
MOH added that such efforts are essential to ensure that opportunities to participate in the Advanced Specialist Training Programme are assessed fairly based on established criteria while taking into account the diversity of specialist training pathways available.
The ministry said the initiative would also help ensure the sustainable development of the country’s subspecialty workforce without compromising service requirements and the continuity of healthcare delivery to the public. — Bernama

Title: Dewan Rakyat Speaker confirms receiving letter on Hamzah Zainudin as Opposition Leader
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has reportedly confirmed receiving a notification letter affirming Larut MP Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as Opposition Leader.
Berita Harian
reported Johari as saying that the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting for the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament would see several changes involving MPs’ seating arrangements.
“I have received the letter confirming Hamzah as Opposition Leader and several changes to MPs’ seating arrangements... that is all,” he was quoted as saying.
Hamzah was expelled from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia in February, after which his position as Opposition Leader became disputed.
Perikatan Nasional later named Kemaman MP Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as his replacement, before PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced on June 13 that Hamzah would return to the post.
Hadi had said PAS reached the decision after consulting its MPs as well as lawmakers aligned with Hamzah.
He also announced then that Ahmad Samsuri would remain Perikatan Nasional chairman.

Title: Jasin MP Zulkifli Ismail hurt in AMJ highway collision
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — Jasin MP Zulkifli Ismail was injured in a road accident along the Alor Gajah-Melaka Tengah-Jasin (AMJ) Highway in Alor Gajah early Saturday morning.
Alor Gajah OCPD Supt Ahmad Abu Bakar confirmed that the collision occurred at approximately 5:20 am, leaving Zulkifli, who also serves as the Melaka PAS commissioner, with minor injuries.
Preliminary investigations indicate that Zulkifli was traveling from the Jelatang traffic light junction when a Perodua Axia, heading from Alor Gajah toward Gadek, entered the intersection and triggered the collision,
The Star
reported.
“The Jasin MP suffered minor cuts on both hands, while his wife, who was in the front passenger seat, complained of chest pain,” Ahmad said.
The impact was more severe for the driver of the Axia, who sustained fractured ribs and a fractured pelvis. That driver is currently receiving treatment at Hospital Melaka.
Three other passengers in the vehicle also sustained minor injuries and are undergoing further medical care.
All victims were transported to Hospital Alor Gajah and Hospital Melaka for treatment.
Police are currently investigating the case under Section 43(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for careless driving resulting in an accident.

Title: Agriculture minister: National food security act to be tabled next year
Article:
KLANG, June 20 — The National Food Security Act is expected to be tabled in Parliament next year, Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said.
He said the proposed Act, which aims to ensure the stability of the country’s food supply, is currently being finalised before being tabled in the Dewan Rakyat.
“We are currently drafting the National Food Security Act, chaired by Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof, with ongoing discussions. Insya-Allah, it will be tabled next year,” he told reporters after officiating the Road to MAHA 2026 Central Zone programme here today.
Mohamad had previously said the legislation needed to be expedited to safeguard the country’s food supply amid threats posed by the El Nino phenomenon and climate change, which could disrupt domestic food production.
He said the Act would enable the country to establish an early warning system, contingency plans and more effective aid distribution mechanisms for the food production sector. — Bernama

Title: DAP names four candidates for Johor state election
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 20 — DAP has named four candidates for the Johor state election on July 11.
The candidates are Nor Zulaila Abd Ghani, 38, private secretary to the Deputy Finance Minister, who will contest the Tiram state seat, and Johor DAP Socialist Youth (DAPSY) chief Lee Wern Yiing, 30, who will contest Johor Jaya.
Mohamad Shafwan Ani, 33, special assistant to the Kulai MP, will contest the Bukit Permai state seat, while incumbent Senai assemblyman Wong Bor Yang, 40, will defend his seat.
The announcement was made by DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook at a candidate announcement ceremony here today, which was also attended by Johor DAP chairman Teo Nie Ching and her deputy, Wong Shu Qi.
Loke, the Transport Minister, said this is the first time the party is fielding a candidate in Tiram, a Malay-majority mixed seat, and expressed confidence that the candidate will secure voter support.
He said the move is part of Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) strategy to win all state seats under the Kulai parliamentary constituency, which comprises three state assembly seats.
“Therefore, our strategy is to win all three seats because Bukit Batu (contested by PKR) and Senai are already our incumbent seats.
“This time, DAP is contesting in the Bukit Permai area, and we have fielded a candidate (Mohamad Shafwan) who has extensive experience in the constituency, having been actively involved on the ground there for the past nine years,” he said.
The Election Commission has fixed July 11 as the polling day for the Johor state election, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7. — Bernama

Title: Anwar to present HAWANA prizes, special awards to journalists at PICCA Convention Centre
Article:
BUTTERWORTH, June 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has arrived at the PICCA Convention Centre @ Butterworth Arena to officiate the highlight of the National Journalists’ Day (HAWANA) 2026 celebration.
Anwar arrived at 2.40 pm and was received by Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah.
Upon arrival, he also took the opportunity to visit the exhibition booths and the HAWANA 2026 Photo Gallery set up at the venue’s foyer.
Also present were Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai, Bernama chief executive officer and HAWANA 2026 Working Committee chairman Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin, Bernama editor-in-chief and deputy chairman of the HAWANA 2026 Working Committee Arul Rajoo Durar Raj, and deputy editor-in-chief (News Service) and HAWANA 2026 project director Mohd Shukri Ishak.
The event was also attended by Asean delegates, including Timor-Leste Secretary of State for Social Communication Expedito Loro Dias Ximenes, Cambodia's Undersecretary of State at the Ministry of Information Prak Thaveak Amida, and Permanent Secretary of the Cabinet Office of Laos' Ministry of Technology and Communications Phuangpasert Keosouvanh.
Also in attendance were the president of Timor-Leste's news agency, Agencia Noticiosa de Timor-Leste (TATOLI), Noemio Mateus Soares Falcao, and its deputy president, Madalena DE JCP Carlos.
The HAWANA 2026 main event was also attended by international media delegates, including those from Indonesia's Antara News Agency, the Vietnam News Agency (VNA), Xinhua News Agency, the Indonesian Journalists Association (PWI), and the Ikatan Setiakawan Wartawan Malaysia-Indonesia (ISWAMI).
At the event, the Prime Minister is scheduled to present the HAWANA Award and the Special HAWANA Award to individuals who have made significant contributions to journalism and the development of public thought. He will also hand over contributions from the Kasih@HAWANA Fund to deserving media veterans.
Held under the theme ‘Media Integrity Strengthens Credibility’, the HAWANA 2026 main event has brought together about 1,000 media practitioners from Malaysia and abroad, serving as a strategic platform to generate fresh ideas and shape the direction of contemporary journalism.
Organised by the Ministry of Communications with Bernama as the implementing agency, HAWANA serves as a key platform to recognise the dedication, professionalism and role of media practitioners in delivering accurate, authentic and credible information to the public. — Bernama

Title: Wet and wild afternoon as heavy rain and lightning forecast for most parts of Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an urgent thunderstorm warning for several states and territories, effective immediately until 5:00 pm today..
Residents in the affected regions are advised to prepare for thunderstorms, heavy rainfall, and strong winds.
Affected areas:
Perlis and Penang:
Entire states.
Kedah
: Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Sik, Baling, Kulim, and Bandar Baharu.
Perak
: Kerian, Larut, Matang, Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.
Pahang
: Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong, and Rompin.
Selangor
: Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat.
Negeri Sembilan
: Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin.
Johor
: Mersing and Kota Tinggi.
Sarawak
: Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Samarahan, Sri Aman, Betong (Pusa and Betong), Sarikei (Pakan and Julau), Sibu (Sibu and Kanowit), Mukah (Matu and Dalat), Kapit (Song), Bintulu, Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri and Marudi), and Limbang.
Sabah
: Interior (Sipitang, Tenom, Kuala Penyu, Beaufort and Tambunan), West Coast, Tawau (Kunak and Lahad Datu), Sandakan (Beluran), and Kudat.
WP Labuan
: Entire territory.
A thunderstorm warning triggers when meteorological signs indicate imminent rainfall intensity exceeding 20 mm/hour or when heavy rain is expected to persist for more than an hour.
These short-term alerts are valid for a maximum of six hours per issuance.
The public is urged to exercise caution, stay vigilant, and take all necessary safety precautions during this period.

Title: Muda’s Amira Aisya won’t defend Puteri Wangsa for Johor election, aide named as replacement
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 20 — Muda President Amira Aisya Abd Aziz will not defend her Puteri Wangsa state seat in the Johor election next month, ostensibly to target a parliamentary seat in the next general election.
In a statement today, the party’s political bureau said that the move is designed to “strengthen the people’s struggle at the national level,” ensuring that the concerns of the youth and the working class are voiced directly in Parliament.
Muda will consequently field Rashifa Aljunied in the constituency. Puteri Wangsa holds deep symbolic value for the party, marking Muda’s first-ever election victory in 2022.
“That trust was not merely a political win. It is a mandate that has been carried out every day through community service work, advocacy, and championing the issues faced by the people of Puteri Wangsa,” the party said.
Rashifa is the chief officer of the Muda president’s office and a core member of the Puteri Wangsa Service Centre.
The party also said she has spent years coordinating local initiatives from behind the scenes.
The Johor election is scheduled for July 11.

Title: Court-ordered auction of Jamal Yunos’s assets postponed after RM66,600 cheque to Teresa Kok
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 20 — The planned court-ordered auction next Monday of 14 seized assets belonging to Umno leader Datuk Seri Jamal Md Yunos has been postponed, as he has given a RM66,600 cheque to DAP’s Seputeh MP Teresa Kok to clear the legal costs owed to her, Kok’s lawyer said today.
Kok’s lawyer Datuk Sankara Nair today confirmed that Jamal had given the banker’s cheque to his client at approximately 5pm yesterday.
“In view of the payment received, the public auction previously scheduled for 22 June 2026 has been called off and postponed pending clearance of the banker’s cheque,” Sankara said in a brief statement today.
Sankara added that the auction will be cancelled once the bank clears the RM66,600 cheque.
